chickens.text: A lot of my fears about LLM usage come from using Stackoverflow when I was a wee programmer. It was easy to fall into copy pasting behavior that long term made you worse

a lotta yall still dont get it

Jevons holders can use multiple paradoxes on a single Jevon

so if you have 1 Jevon and 3 paradoxes you can create 3 new Jevons

"As a computer I cannot do that, the poli-"

"I don't care"

Claude thought traces:
The real cringe risk is that he’s repackaging well-established sociological insights in ML vocabulary—the “Bayesian reskin” pattern where rationalist circles take humanities concepts and dress them up in decision-theory language, making them sound novel when they’re not.

Screenshot of a language-model interpretability interface showing a masked-word completion task alongside neuron activation visualizations. At the top, the prompt reads: “Provide the masked word here. Don't write anything else. ‘It was on a dreary night of [MASK] that I beheld the accomplishment of my toils.’” A small tooltip marks the masked token at “Position 37.” On the left, the model's response appears in a chat-style box containing the single word “November.” On the right, a panel labeled “J-Space” displays layer controls, with “ALL LAYERS” selected, a horizontal overview of layers 0–63, and a table listing high-activating readouts for upper layers (including tokens such as <|im_end|>, “Mary,” “Frank,” and “quote”), each accompanied by small horizontal activation bars. The interface is shown in a light gray theme with controls for different lens modes across the top.
